# #1165 - wrong results of union_ with double and no rescaling [Closed]

> Username: BenoitAndrieu  
> Created at: 2023-06-21 10:09:48 UTC  
> Updated at: 2023-09-14 08:15:00 UTC  
> Closed at: 2023-09-14 08:14:59 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165  

Hi, I am posting some results I have with using `double`.  
They prevent me to use `double`.  
Thanks for any hints.  
By the way, boost::geometry is great ! :heart:  
  
The results are fine if using :  
- `float` with or without `BOOST_GEOMETRY_ROBUSTNESS_ALTERNATIVE`  
- `double` with `BOOST_GEOMETRY_ROBUSTNESS_ALTERNATIVE`  
  
```  
#include <boost/geometry.hpp>  
#include <gtest/gtest.h>  
  
using boost_coordinate_t = double;  
using boost_point_2d = boost::geometry::model::d2::point_xy<boost_coordinate_t>;  
using boost_polygon_2d = boost::geometry::model::polygon<boost_point_2d>;  
using boost_multipolygon_2d = boost::geometry::model::multi_polygon<boost_polygon_2d>;  
```

---

TEST(TestCaseName, UnionCheckCase006)  
{  
	auto one = boost::geometry::from_wkt<boost_multipolygon_2d>("MULTIPOLYGON(((0.496347946586042088057411092449910938739776611328125 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125,0.4963479465860423101020160174812190234661102294921875 -0.615434933232551717452452066936530172824859619140625,0.8163479465860421502299004714586772024631500244140625 -0.9354349332325515575803365209139883518218994140625,0.81634794658604192818529554642736911773681640625 -4.4754349332325507049290536087937653064727783203125,0.69634794658604182160388518241234123706817626953125 -4.4754349332325507049290536087937653064727783203125,0.69634794658604182160388518241234123706817626953125 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125,0.496347946586042088057411092449910938739776611328125 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125)))");  
	auto one_area = boost::geometry::area(one);  
  
	auto two = boost::geometry::from_wkt<boost_polygon_2d>("POLYGON((0.496347946586041810501654936160775832831859588623046875 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125,0.476347946586041792738086542158271186053752899169921875 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125,0.476347946586042014782691467189579270780086517333984375 -0.5954349332325516996888836729340255260467529296875,0.496347946586042032546259861192083917558193206787109375 -0.615434933232551717452452066936530172824859619140625,0.496347946586041810501654936160775832831859588623046875 -4.57543493323255034965768572874367237091064453125))");  
	auto two_area = boost::geometry::area(two);  
  
	boost_multipolygon_2d union_result;  
	boost::geometry::union_(one, two, union_result);  
	auto union_area = boost::geometry::area(union_result);  
  
	EXPECT_EQ(union_result.size(), 1);  
}

	auto two_area = boost::geometry::area(two);  
  
	boost_multipolygon_2d union_result;  
	boost::geometry::union_(one, two, union_result);  
	auto union_area = boost::geometry::area(union_result);  
  
	EXPECT_EQ(union_result.size(), 1);  
}

## Comment 6

> Username: barendgehrels  
> Created at: 2023-06-25 10:11:26 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165#issuecomment-1606005881  

Hi, thanks for the report and the :heart: !  
  
I checked your first case `UnionCheckCase005` and I don't see anything wrong with it.  
Apart from that, it works the same for `float`, `double` and `long double`  
  
This is what I added:  
```  
	std::cout  
		<< " area 1: " << one_area  
		<< " area 2: " << two_area   
		<< " area: " << union_area   
		<< " size: " << union_result.size()   
		<< " valid: " << boost::geometry::is_valid(union_result)  
		<< std::endl;  
```  
and it reports `area 1: 65.9486 area 2: 26.6959 area: 92.6445 size: 8 valid: 1`  
  
So your first test expects 7 polygons, but there are 8, and that's what I count as well.  
  
I also added a visualization, similar to you but with qgis, and this which looks correct to me.  
  
  
![image](https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/assets/334849/7a281b12-80e6-4c0d-990b-490e07bdffe9)  
  
I didn't investigate the other issues - can you first check which cases are really invalid and why you think so?

---

## Comment 8

> Username: barendgehrels  
> Created at: 2023-06-28 08:43:35 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165#issuecomment-1611010170  

I now see what you mean. I looked in more detail and I don't think they should be merged. They are so close together that pictures don't help here. I tried with Boost Multiprecision and there are still eight. Then I run this loop:  
```  
	for (std::size_t i = 0; i < union_result.size(); i++)		  
	{  
		for (std::size_t j = i + 1; j < union_result.size(); j++)		  
		{  
			std::cout << "Mutual distance: " << i << " " << j << " " << boost::geometry::distance(union_result[i], union_result[j]) << std::endl;  
			  
		}  
	}  
  
```  
and I get (partly):  
```  
Mutual distance: 2 6 0.07  
Mutual distance: 2 7 2.66454e-15  
Mutual distance: 3 4 1.12219  
```  
  
with this figure, with indices of polygons of the multi-polygon:  
![image](https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/assets/334849/7ebc35f7-fc9c-44c7-9233-657f1424061c)  
  
So you expect 2 and 7 to merge, but they are `2.66454e-15` units apart.

---

## Comment 9

> Username: barendgehrels  
> Created at: 2023-06-28 08:45:46 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165#issuecomment-1611013074  

With `float` they indeed merge. But that is a short coming of float.

## Comment 10

> Username: BenoitAndrieu  
> Created at: 2023-06-28 15:03:08 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165#issuecomment-1611611523  

Hi, thanks for the update.  
I am not entirely surprised if the polygons are not intersecting but they are so near that I expect/would very much like them to merge.  
  
What would you recommend to make it "work" ?  
  
I think it will be the same with UnionCheckCase006 and UnionCheckCase008  
UnionCheckCase007 and DiffCheckCase001 looks more buggy

---

## Comment 11

> Username: barendgehrels  
> Created at: 2023-07-02 08:39:38 UTC  
> Url: https://github.com/boostorg/geometry/issues/1165#issuecomment-1616457458  

There are some options you could try  
  
* live with it, the behavior is correct (at least for the first case that I verified)  
* go back to float  
* round coordinates yourself, for example up to the sixth decimal  
* apply a buffer around each of your geometries with a distance of, for example, 3.0e-15 (this would probably solve your first case)  
  
Probably none of them is ideal.  
  
You can also try to merge them with an own algorithm, for example by finding very close points and omit these borders. That would not be too hard for your first case. But of course, for other cases it might become quite complex. We don't offer this algorithm...

---

> Username: BenoitAndrieu

Should I close the issue and I create a new one with only the cases UnionCheckCase007 and DiffCheckCase001 ?
